info.name: Bifrost Model Catalog Wiring
info.description: End-to-end wiring tests between the management API and the model catalog. Each scenario stands up an isolated, run-namespaced custom provider backed by a real upstream (OpenAI, Anthropic, or Gemini), mutates its providers/keys, and asserts the catalog read endpoints reflect each mutation. Reads that depend on the asynchronously populated live-model cache poll with exponential backoff. Machine-generated by runners/build-model-catalog-wiring.mjs — do not hand-edit.

exec: pm.test('setup: list providers', function () { pm.expect(pm.response.code, pm.response.text()).to.equal(200); });
pm.test('setup: delete provider', function () { pm.expect([200, 204, 404], 'status ' + pm.response.code + ' body ' + pm.response.text()).to.include(pm.response.code); });

item.name: Add provider and key surfaces models (openai)
item.description: A fresh provider plus one gated key surfaces that key's allowed model in the catalog.

exec: if (pm.response.code !== 200) { throw new Error('list models status ' + pm.response.code); }
if (expectEmpty && names.length !== 0) { throw new Error('expected no models for ' + providerName + ' but got ' + JSON.stringify(names)); }
if (expectNonEmpty && names.length === 0) { throw new Error('expected a non-empty catalog for ' + providerName); }
if (names.indexOf(m) < 0) { throw new Error('expected model ' + m + ' in ' + JSON.stringify(names)); }
if (!captured) { throw new Error('captured variable ' + v + ' is empty'); }
if (names.indexOf(m) >= 0) { throw new Error('expected model ' + m + ' absent but found in ' + JSON.stringify(names)); }
try { assertNow(); } catch (e) { ok = false; errMsg = e.message; }
pm.test("03. key model appears in catalog [openai-add-provider-and-key]", function () { pm.expect(true, 'assertion satisfied').to.be.true; });
var start = Date.now(); while (Date.now() - start < sleepMs) {}
pm.test("03. key model appears in catalog [openai-add-provider-and-key]", function () { throw new Error(errMsg); });

item.name: Disabled key drops models, re-enable restores (openai)
item.description: A disabled key is skipped during aggregation; re-enabling it brings its models back.

item.name: Alias resolves to underlying model at inference (openai)
item.description: A key alias routes an inference request to the underlying wire model rather than being rejected.

exec: if (pm.response.code !== 200) { throw new Error('list models status ' + pm.response.code); }
if (expectEmpty && names.length !== 0) { throw new Error('expected no models for ' + providerName + ' but got ' + JSON.stringify(names)); }

try { assertNow(); } catch (e) { ok = false; errMsg = e.message; }
pm.test("03. aliased key model present [openai-alias-resolution]", function () { pm.expect(true, 'assertion satisfied').to.be.true; });
var start = Date.now(); while (Date.now() - start < sleepMs) {}
pm.test("03. aliased key model present [openai-alias-resolution]", function () { throw new Error(errMsg); });
if (pm.response.code !== 200) { throw new Error('inference status ' + pm.response.code + ' body ' + pm.response.text()); }
if (!body.choices || body.choices.length === 0) { throw new Error('inference returned no choices'); }
if (used.indexOf("gpt-4o-mini") < 0) { throw new Error('expected resolved model gpt-4o-mini in response.model=' + used); }
try { assertNow(); } catch (e) { ok = false; errMsg = e.message; }
pm.test("04. alias resolves to underlying model [openai-alias-resolution]", function () { pm.expect(true, 'assertion satisfied').to.be.true; });
var start = Date.now(); while (Date.now() - start < sleepMs) {}
pm.test("04. alias resolves to underlying model [openai-alias-resolution]", function () { throw new Error(errMsg); });

item.name: Blacklisting a model re-gates a wildcard catalog (openai)
item.description: A wildcard key surfaces the upstream's live model list; adding one of those models to the key's blacklist drops it from the catalog while the rest of the list stays. The target model is captured from the live list at run time because some upstreams only report dated ids.

exec: if (pm.response.code !== 200) { throw new Error('list models status ' + pm.response.code); }
if (!hit) { throw new Error('no live model with prefix ' + prefix + ' in ' + JSON.stringify(names.slice(0, 20))); }
try { assertNow(); } catch (e) { ok = false; errMsg = e.message; }
pm.test("03. wildcard catalog serves live models [openai-blacklist-regate]", function () { pm.expect(true, 'assertion satisfied').to.be.true; });
var start = Date.now(); while (Date.now() - start < sleepMs) {}
pm.test("03. wildcard catalog serves live models [openai-blacklist-regate]", function () { throw new Error(errMsg); });

item.name: Catalog aggregates the union across enabled keys (openai)
item.description: Two keys gating distinct models both contribute: the catalog lists the union of their allow-lists.

item.name: Model details endpoint respects the key gate (openai)
item.description: /api/models/details lists only the models the provider's keys allow, like the plain list endpoint.

item.name: Base model list is unaffected by key changes (openai)
item.description: /api/models/base reflects the datasheet's distinct base names; removing a model from a key's allow-list must not remove its base name from that list.

if (used.indexOf("gemini-2.5-flash-lite") < 0) { throw new Error('expected resolved model gemini-2.5-flash-lite in response.model=' + used); }
try { assertNow(); } catch (e) { ok = false; errMsg = e.message; }
pm.test("04. alias resolves to underlying model [gemini-alias-resolution]", function () { pm.expect(true, 'assertion satisfied').to.be.true; });
var start = Date.now(); while (Date.now() - start < sleepMs) {}
pm.test("04. alias resolves to underlying model [gemini-alias-resolution]", function () { throw new Error(errMsg); });

item.name: Blacklisting a model re-gates a wildcard catalog (gemini)
item.description: A wildcard key surfaces the upstream's live model list; adding one of those models to the key's blacklist drops it from the catalog while the rest of the list stays. The target model is captured from the live list at run time because some upstreams only report dated ids.

exec: if (pm.response.code !== 200) { throw new Error('list models status ' + pm.response.code); }
if (!hit) { throw new Error('no live model with prefix ' + prefix + ' in ' + JSON.stringify(names.slice(0, 20))); }
try { assertNow(); } catch (e) { ok = false; errMsg = e.message; }
pm.test("03. wildcard catalog serves live models [gemini-blacklist-regate]", function () { pm.expect(true, 'assertion satisfied').to.be.true; });
var start = Date.now(); while (Date.now() - start < sleepMs) {}
pm.test("03. wildcard catalog serves live models [gemini-blacklist-regate]", function () { throw new Error(errMsg); });

exec: if (pm.response.code !== 200) { throw new Error('list models status ' + pm.response.code); }

item.name: Unreachable list-models upstream keeps key allow-list models
item.description: A provider whose base_url points at a dead port cannot complete live model discovery; the catalog still surfaces the models explicitly allowed by its keys.

item.name: Disallowed list-models blocks discovery but not explicit allow-lists
item.description: A provider whose allowed_requests excludes list_models performs no live discovery: a wildcard key surfaces no catalog rows, while a key with an explicit allow-list still surfaces its models.
